bridge-type.label: Jellyfin Server
bridge-type.description:
Represents a running Jellyfin server instance.

The server bridge connects to the Jellyfin HTTP API and discovers active client sessions. Each discovered session becomes a Jellyfin Client thing.

parameter-group.name: clientDiscoveryFilters
parameter-group.label: Client Discovery Filters
parameter-group.description:
Enable or disable automatic discovery for specific Jellyfin client categories.
The category is determined by the client application name as reported to the Jellyfin server.
Note: These settings only affect the openHAB Inbox; they do not remove already-discovered things.

parameter.name: serverName
parameter.label: Server Name
parameter.description:
Human-readable display name for this Jellyfin server.

If left empty, the name is automatically retrieved from the server upon connection.

parameter.name: hostname
parameter.label: Hostname / IP Address
parameter.description: Hostname or IP address of the Jellyfin server (e.g. 192.168.1.10 or jellyfin.local).

parameter.name: port
parameter.label: Port
parameter.description:
HTTP or HTTPS port of the Jellyfin server.

The default HTTP port is 8096. When Use HTTPS is enabled, use the HTTPS port instead (typically 8920).

parameter.name: ssl
parameter.label: Use HTTPS (Experimental)
parameter.description:
EXPERIMENTAL — Connect to the Jellyfin server using HTTPS instead of plain HTTP.

Enable this when the server is behind a reverse proxy with a valid TLS certificate. Make sure the Port is set to the corresponding HTTPS port.

This feature has not been fully tested. Your feedback is welcome!

parameter.name: path
parameter.label: Base Path
parameter.description:
Optional URL path prefix when the Jellyfin server is hosted behind a reverse proxy at a sub-path.

Example: /jellyfin if the server is reachable at https://myhost/jellyfin. Leave empty for root-hosted installations.

parameter.name: refreshSeconds
parameter.label: Refresh Interval
parameter.description:
How often (in seconds) the binding polls the Jellyfin server for the current playback state of all connected clients.

Lower values provide more responsive state updates but increase server load. The default of 30 s is suitable for most setups.

parameter.name: token
parameter.label: Access Token
parameter.description:
API access token used to authenticate with the Jellyfin server.

Generate a token in the Jellyfin web interface under Dashboard → API Keys. If left empty, the binding operates without authentication (only suitable for servers with public access enabled).

parameter.name: discoverWebClients
parameter.label: Discover Web Clients
parameter.description:
Discover Jellyfin Web clients.
Matches clients reporting as: Jellyfin Web

parameter.name: discoverAndroidClients
parameter.label: Discover Android Clients
parameter.description:
Discover Jellyfin Android clients.
Matches clients reporting as: Jellyfin for Android

parameter.name: discoverAndroidTvClients
parameter.label: Discover Android TV Clients
parameter.description:
Discover Jellyfin Android TV clients.
Matches clients reporting as: Jellyfin for Android TV

parameter.name: discoverIosClients
parameter.label: Discover iOS Clients
parameter.description:
Discover iOS clients.
Matches clients reporting as: Jellyfin iOS, Swiftfin, Infuse

parameter.name: discoverKodiClients
parameter.label: Discover Kodi Clients
parameter.description:
Discover Kodi clients.
Matches clients reporting as: JellyCon, Jellyfin for Kodi

parameter.name: discoverRokuClients
parameter.label: Discover Roku Clients
parameter.description:
Discover Roku clients.
Matches clients reporting as: Jellyfin for Roku

parameter.name: discoverOtherClients
parameter.label: Discover Other Clients
parameter.description: Discover third-party clients not matched by any other category above.
